description: tty-theme — PRD v1.2 implementation. Completes remaining CLI commands (list, export, browse, publish, share, gallery-install), community gallery API endpoints, and removes the deprecated image mode code.

userStories.title: Remove image mode code and --image / --refine flags from CLI
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to delete modes/image_mode.py, the image/ directory, and remove --image/--refine options from cli/main.py so the codebase matches PRD v1.2 (prompt-only).
acceptanceCriteria: Remove --image and --refine options from cli/main.py generate command
Remove any import of image_mode or image.* from cli/main.py and api/main.py
Remove test_image_mode.py and test_image_loader.py from tests/
uv run pytest passes (pre-existing failures in test_llm.py and test_api.py are acceptable)
userStories.notes: PRD v1.2 drops image mode entirely. Clean removal only.

userStories.title: Add slug generation utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a shared make_slug() function so CLI publish and API endpoints produce consistent URL-safe slugs.
acceptanceCriteria: Add make_slug(name: str) -> str in generator/slug.py
Lowercases, replaces spaces/underscores with hyphens, strips non-alphanumeric chars except hyphens
Collapses consecutive hyphens, strips leading/trailing hyphens
userStories.notes: Foundation for publish, install, share, and gallery API.

userStories.title: Add `tty-theme list` command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to list all locally cached themes so I can see what I have without searching.
acceptanceCriteria: Calls repo.list_themes(limit) and prints each theme: created_at[:16], source, query_raw or name (truncated to 40 chars)
Prints helpful message when cache is empty

userStories.title: Add `tty-theme export` command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to export a cached theme by name to a specific target format so I can convert a Ghostty-cached theme to iTerm2.
acceptanceCriteria: Accepts positional THEME_NAME argument and --target flag (default 'ghostty')
Searches repo.list_themes() for a theme whose name or query_raw contains THEME_NAME (case-insensitive)
Serializes theme_data through the correct ThemeSerializer and prints to stdout
Exits with error if no match found

userStories.title: Add TTY_THEME_API_URL constant to cli/main.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all remote API calls to use a single TTY_THEME_API_URL env var so users can point at self-hosted instances.
acceptanceCriteria: Add _API_BASE = os.environ.get('TTY_THEME_API_URL', 'https://api.tty-theme.dev') near top of cli/main.py
All subsequent CLI commands that call the remote API use _API_BASE
userStories.notes: Must be added before browse/publish/install use it.

userStories.title: Add gallery browse endpoint GET /v1/themes to API
userStories.description: As a web or CLI user, I want to browse community themes via the API so the gallery can be populated.
acceptanceCriteria: Accepts query params: sort ('newest' | 'downloads' | 'likes', default 'newest'), limit (int, default 20, max 100), offset (int, default 0)
Returns JSON: { themes: [{id, slug, name, source, provider, created_at, theme_data}], total, offset }
slug is make_slug(name) for each theme

userStories.title: Add gallery publish endpoint POST /v1/themes to API
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to publish a theme to the community gallery via API.
acceptanceCriteria: Request body: { name: str, theme_data: str, source: str = 'community', provider: str | None }
Validates theme_data with validate_theme() — returns 422 on failure
Returns 409 if a theme with that slug already exists

userStories.title: Add gallery get-by-slug endpoint GET /v1/themes/:slug to API
userStories.description: As a CLI install command, I want to fetch a theme by slug so I can install it locally.
acceptanceCriteria: Iterates list_themes() and returns the first theme where make_slug(name) == slug
Returns { id, slug, name, theme_data, source, provider, created_at } or 404

userStories.title: Add `tty-theme browse` command
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to browse community themes from the gallery without opening a browser.
acceptanceCriteria: Calls GET {_API_BASE}/v1/themes via httpx with a 5s timeout
If API unreachable, falls back to local repo.list_themes() with a stderr notice
Prints table: slug (30 chars), source, created_at[:10]

userStories.title: Add `tty-theme publish` command
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to publish a locally cached theme to the community gallery.
acceptanceCriteria: Looks up theme in local cache (same substring match as export)

userStories.title: Add `tty-theme install <slug>` command
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to install a community theme by slug directly from the gallery.
acceptanceCriteria: Add @app.command() 'install' to cli/main.py (separate from --install flag on generate)
On success: calls _install_theme() with returned theme_data and prints install path + activation hint
On 404: prints 'Theme <slug> not found. Browse themes with: tty-theme browse'

userStories.title: Add `tty-theme share` command
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want a shareable URL for a locally cached theme.
acceptanceCriteria: Searches local cache for theme by name/query_raw substring
If theme not found locally, prints error with suggestion to generate it first
Adds hint: 'Run tty-theme publish <name> to make it available to others'

userStories.title: Add neofetch-style endpoint GET /v1/neofetch/:slug to API
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a text/plain ANSI block for any theme so it can be embedded in terminal READMEs.
acceptanceCriteria: Add GET /v1/neofetch/{slug} to api/main.py returning text/plain
Parses theme_data key=value to extract palette colors 0-15 plus background/foreground
Outputs: theme name on first line, then two rows of 8 colored blocks using ANSI \x1b[48;2;R;G;Bm  \x1b[0m for each palette color
Returns 404 plain text if slug not found

userStories.title: Show spend and key status in `tty-theme config status`
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want config status to show today's spend and which provider keys are configured.
acceptanceCriteria: Shows today's total: repo.get_daily_spend() formatted as '$X.XXXX'
For each provider in ['gemini','groq','openai','anthropic','mistral']: shows '  <name>  ✓ configured' or '  <name>  - not set' by checking keyring

userStories.title: Remove image tab from mockup.html, add gallery browse section
userStories.description: As a product stakeholder, I want mockup.html to reflect PRD v1.2: no image tab, prompt-only, with a community gallery preview.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the 'Image' tab button and #panel-image section entirely from mockup.html
Remove all JS related to image upload: drop zone handlers, file input, URL input, ondrop, ondragover
The prompt tab is now the only active tab (remove tab bar if only one tab remains)
Add a '── Community Themes ──' section below the generator with 3 hardcoded placeholder theme cards (name, swatch strip of 8 colored divs, 'tty-theme install <slug>' chip)
Gallery section has a 'Browse all →' link pointing to '#gallery'
userStories.notes: CLAUDE.md: mockup.html stays in sync with PRD.
